titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Kelly Besser, 2026.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated 2026 March 11 by Caroline Cubé.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-03-11 15:20:57 -0700.

physdesc.extent: (10 unprocessed open reel audiotapes and 1 unprocessed CD-R)
(1 unprocessed CD-R, and 10 unprocessed jaz drives)

did.abstract: Gloria Richetta Jones was born in Cincinnati, Ohio in 1945. Her family moved to Los Angeles when she was seven years old, where she took up classical piano and sang gospel music locally at The Church of God in Christ. When she was 14, she helped form her first gospel singing group which included organist Billy Preston; future gospel superstar Andraé Crouch and his twin sister Sandra Crouch; a pair of future Motown artists, Sondra "Blinky" Williams and Franie Kahrl; and Edna Wright, who would become the lead singer of 70s soul trio Honey Cone. The group named themselves The Cogics (after The Church of God in Christ) and recorded the LP "It's A Blessing" which was released in 1964. Jones also sang background vocals at a variety of Los Angeles recording studios, including Motown's West Coast branch, where she wrote, produced, and arranged songs. The collection documents aspects of Jones's personal life and music career and consists of photographs, notes, correspondence, sheet music, financial records, jaz drives, and audiovisual materials.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. Advance notice is required for access to the collection. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: CONTAINS UNPROCESSED AUDIO and DIGITAL MATERIALS: Audio and digital materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Property rights to the objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Gloria Jones papers (Collection 2409). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's library collections and archives.

bioghist.head: Biographical
bioghist.p: Born in Cincinnati, Ohio in 1945, Gloria Richetta Jones and her family moved to Los Angeles when she was seven years old, where she took up classical piano and sang gospel music locally at The Church of God in Christ. At the age of 14, she helped form her first gospel singing group which included organist Billy Preston; future gospel superstar Andraé Crouch and his twin sister Sandra Crouch; a pair of future Motown artists, Sondra "Blinky" Williams and Franie Kahrl; and Edna Wright, who would become the lead singer of 70s soul trio Honey Cone. The group named themselves The Cogics (after The Church of God in Christ) and recorded the LP "It's A Blessing" which was released in 1964. Jones also sang background vocals at a variety of Los Angeles recording studios, including Motown's West Coast branch where she wrote, produced, and arranged songs.

In the late 60s, Jones joined the Los Angeles production of Hair, where she met Bolan of T. Rex when T. Rex was on the verge of rock and roll stardom. In 1973, Jones moved to England to join Bolan as background singer and keyboardist for T. Rex. Bolan encouraged Jones to restart her solo career, especially after the success of her recording of "Tainted Love." Bolan produced her 1976 album Vixen which combined soul and rock elements in another eclectic outing, including two different versions of T. Rex's "Get It On," an achingly slow version of Bessie Banks's "Go Now," the Motown-ish "Would You Like To Know," and an updated version of "Tainted Love." Tragically, shortly thereafter, Bolan died in a car crash with Jones at the wheel, leaving her with their son. She returned to California to record the album Windstorm and dedicated it to him. After the boost she received from Soft Cell's "Tainted Love" cover, she recorded periodically, including a reunion album with The Cogics in 1984. More recently, Gloria Jones has been involved with establishing and building the Marc Bolan School of Music in Sierra Leone, teaching music to the youth of the West African nation.

Rolan Bolan, only two years old when his father died, grew up in the music industry and continues to develop his talent. He has recorded with various artists, performing both original music and T. Rex songs. He earned a degree in recording arts from Loyola Marymount University. Determined not to cash in on his father's name, he initially used his father's birth surname, Feld. He took a series of jobs, including working in a jewelry store, modeling for Tommy Hilfiger, and playing gigs both domestically and abroad. His band, Rolan Bolan and The Brothers Bounce, is documented in the collection with photographs. Currently, he works as an actor and musician and produced the re-recorded version of T. Rex's Children of the Revolution amongst a multitude of other projects.
Source: Johnson Rare Books and Archives. Invoice correspondence, 2021.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gloria Jones is an African American singer and songwriter from Los Angeles. The materials were created by Jones and her son Rolan Bolan. The materials document aspects of Jones's personal life and music career. The collection consists of photographs, notes, correspondence, sheet music, financial records, jaz drives, and audiovisual materials.

A collection of material documenting the personal life and musical career of Gloria Jones, soul singer, actress, and songwriter and mother of Rolan Seymour Feld (Bolan), son of British rock star Marc Bolan of T. Rex fame. The collection includes a selection of photographs, legal papers, documents involving Jones's recording contracts and royalties, some correspondence, handwritten notes and lyrics, as well as master tape recordings on Jaz disc drives. A portion of this collection also documents the early musical, acting, and modeling career of her son.
Source: Johnson Rare Books and Archives. Invoice correspondence, 2021.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection was arranged by The Book Shop.

did.unittitle: Professional and family photographs and ephemera
Red Tomahawk Records invoice and Kwaito From Township To International Airwaves script
Clippings, publicity, and professional head shot
Mark Feld certified birth certificate copy

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: CONTAINS UNPROCESSED AUDIO and DIGITAL MATERIALS: Audio and digital materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.

Box contains 10 jaz drives with the following titles: Hair Down, The Pepper #2, Tasha Taylor #4 Stand Bkup, Operator, American Tramps, Low Life, The Pepper, Possibilities, Present Tenses, and Lonely Eyes.
Box contains 2 CD-Rs entitled Gloria Jones Share My Love + Bonus Tracks and Rolan Bolan photos - Silverlake Lounge.

did.unittitle: Gloria Jones portraits and open reel audiotapes

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: BOX CONTAINS UNPROCESSED AUDIO MATERIALS: Audio materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.
Box contains two 1/4 inch open reel audiotapes with the following tracks: Could have been Fun, Feel the chill again, I'm Your Lucky Number, and Kiss me, Kiss me.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: BOX CONTAIN UNPROCESSED AUDIO MATERIALS: Audio materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.
Box contains three 1/4 inch open reel audiotapes with the following tracks: (My Main Squeeze) Squeezed Me Out, Why Can't You Love Only Me, Dancing in the Corner, and Is He My Angel.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: BOX CONTAIN UNPROCESSED AUDIO MATERIALS: Audio materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.
Box contains three 1/2 inch open reel audiotapes with the following tracks: Liberty Man and You Can Be A Star.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: BOX CONTAIN UNPROCESSED AUDIO MATERIALS: Audio materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.
Box 8 contains one 2 inch open reel audiotape and Box 9 contains one 2 inch open reel audiotape entitled Planet Swan with the following tracks: Arieta Rome and Y.O.U.
